channel.title: New local problems on FixMyStreet
channel.description: The latest local problems reported by users

item.title: Yellow Lines not completed, 17th May
item.description:
Double yellow lines was ment to be painted to allow space and acess for fire engines to get thorough but a big gap has been left which allows big vehicles to park and block the road
Nearest address to the pin placed on the map (from OS Places API): Barbot Close, Edmonton
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Dangerous fence Screws., 17th May
item.description:
There is a plastic fence protecting access to the Thames where a length of wooden fencing is damaged. It is best described as on mud marks way, the path near Greenwich Yacht Club walking towards the 02. My 6 year old son nearly had a screw embedded in his head, he is autistic. A contractor or council is very much aware that there is danger as there is a plastic fence, but how the hell do they leave 2 inch screws upwards, common sense defies people! I work as an electrician and it’s safety, safety, safety. Make it safe, leave it safe - if it’s not safe - make it safe. My point being, my son could have had a screw through his head today, it’s only by chance that I saw the screws and placed my hand in front of them before his head made contact. This needs addressing, quickly.

Nearest address to the pin placed on the map (from OS Places API): Peartree Way, Greenwich
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: 40 miles limit on A1 when trucks hit this very loud sound, 17th May
item.description:
This is a pothole on A1 due to the drainage. Very loud sound in nearby blocks when trucks hit this pothole every day constantly

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Lyttelton Road, Hampstead Garden Suburb, Greater London

item.title: These are left in here for more than 2 months and no one has come to pick them. Is really hard to walk on that path especially if you have a child or to take out the bins for collection., 17th May
item.description:
These are left in here for more than 2 months and no one has come to pick them. Is really hard to walk on that path especially if you have a child or to take out the bins for collection.

Hasn't been cleaned ever - poor animals eating the dirt (see photo).

item.title: Flytipping in alleyway, 17th May
item.description:
Looks like there's possible building materials (plumbing) as well as general rubbish and cupboard. Have got more photo evidence than stated (incl address) . Only discovered today (17.05.26). Located in the alleyway off of Halfway Street, behind Longmeadow Road.

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): 193, Halfway Street, Longlands, Sidcup, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Lots of dog waste outside Goodrich School, 17th May
item.description:
I think the foxes have been going at a litter bin and have left lots of rubbish, including plenty of dog poo bags, outside the school (Goodrich School by the Dunstans Road entrance.)

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): 145, Dunstan's Road, East Dulwich, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Very deep potholes, 17th May
item.description:
Reporting again as nothing has been done to address the very deep potholes near the farm and towards the brow of the hill.please can this be fixed a.s.a.p as i feel i may have to charge you for a replacement tyre or to have the suspension done on my car as everytime i drive up shooter's hill my right tyre's goes down it with quite a force.
Nearest address to the pin placed on the map (from OS Places API): Shooters Hill, Plumstead
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Garden waste dumped 16/05/2026, 17th May
item.description:
They've butchered the hedge during nesting season and have left it all over the pavement, blocking access to pedestrians. It has been there since yesterday.

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Rangefield Road, Sundridge, Greater London

item.title: Overflowing bin, rubbish all over pavement, 17th May
item.description:
Public health hazard; litter and food waste on pavement.
Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Albemarle Road, Shortlands, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Request for Basketball Court Markings to Support Youth Activity and Community Wellbeing, 17th May
item.description:
Dear Bexley Council, My name is Riley, and I am writing on behalf of a group of local young people aged 13–16, including myself, Krists, Jayden, Martin, Tommy, James, Ojacob, Uphaz, Robertus, CJ, Finley, Henry, Arjun, Kylan and Ashad. We regularly use the local basketball court, and we would like to kindly request the addition of 3‑point lines and free‑throw markings. At the moment, the court is usable, but without proper markings it is difficult for us to practise real basketball skills or play proper games. Adding these lines would make a huge difference to us and many others in the community. Why this would benefit our area Encourages exercise: Clear court markings would motivate more young people to come outside, be active, and spend time away from screens. Basketball is one of the few sports that is free, accessible, and easy for anyone to join in. Supports mental health: Physical activity is proven to reduce stress and improve mood. Having a proper court gives young people a positive outlet, especially during stressful school years. Builds a stronger community: A marked court would bring together people of different ages and backgrounds. It creates a safe, positive space where friendships can grow and where young people can socialise in a healthy way. Promotes skill development: Many of us are passionate about basketball and want to improve. Having proper lines allows us to practise shooting, spacing, teamwork and real game situations. A small improvement with a big impact We believe this is a simple, low‑cost upgrade that would have a long‑lasting positive effect on our community. It would show young people that the council supports us and wants to invest in our wellbeing. Thank you for taking the time to read our request. We would be very grateful if the council could consider adding these markings, and we would be happy to discuss this further if needed. Kind regards, Riley Peter Goodridge (14 years old) Belvedere resident On behalf of local young people aged 13–16
Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Parkside Road, Lower Belvedere, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Bike stand bollards, 17th May
item.description:
On Marcon Place at the bottom by the Amherst Road entrance with a hire bike bay. The two bollards have come out of the road and laying in the road. Bolts not hiding into tarmac

Nearest address to the pin placed on the map (from OS Places API): Amhurst Road, Hackney, London
Report on FixMyStreet
